mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months signals rising inflation expectations, leading to a decrease in the purchasing power of consumers and a subsequent decrease in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not decrease by more than 1% in response to a 2% CPI increase, or the correlation between CPI and S&P 500 returns remains positive over the next 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over the past 3 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to an increase in wages and a subsequent increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not increase by more than 1% in response to a 0.5% UNRATE decrease, or the correlation between UNRATE and S&P 500 returns remains negative over the next 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over the past 6 months signals an increase in economic activity, leading to an increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not increase by more than 1% in response to a 2% INDPRO increase, or the correlation between INDPRO and S&P 500 returns remains negative over the next 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not decrease by more than 1% in response to a 1% FEDFUNDS increase, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS and S&P 500 returns remains positive over the next 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decrease in the RSAFS (Retail Sales) over the past 3 months signals a decrease in consumer spending, leading to a decrease in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not decrease by more than 1% in response to a 1% RSAFS decrease, or the correlation between RSAFS and S&P 500 returns remains positive over the next 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the PAYEMS (Nonfarm Payroll Employment) over the past 3 months signals an increase in labor market activity, leading to an increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not increase by more than 1% in response to a 1% PAYEMS increase, or the correlation between PAYEMS and S&P 500 returns remains negative over the next 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the HOUST (Housing Starts) over the past 3 months signals an increase in housing market activity, leading to an increase in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not increase by more than 1% in response to a 1% HOUST increase, or the correlation between HOUST and S&P 500 returns remains negative over the next 2 years.
